items.title: F-018: Android App
items.intent: Expand to Android to serve the broader market — must be native Kotlin, not a lowest-common-denominator port
items.description: Native Android app using Kotlin with native UI. Shared logic via KMP or Rust. Must feel native to Android — Material Design, Android navigation patterns, system file picker integration.
acceptanceCriteria: Android app feels native — an Android user would not guess it was ported from iOS
Opens any .md file via Android file picker
Local AI works on supported Android devices

items.title: F-019: Linux Desktop App
items.intent: Linux developers are our people — they write markdown daily, they value open formats, and they are completely underserved by native markdown editors
items.description: Native Linux desktop app. Tech approach: evaluate GTK4 with Rust or Kotlin Multiplatform. Shared logic (parser, formatter, AI) from Swift codebase via Rust bridge or rewrite. Must feel native to Linux — follows GNOME HIG or provides distro-appropriate theming. Full file system access. Local AI via llama.cpp or ONNX Runtime (no Core ML on Linux).
acceptanceCriteria: App feels native on Linux — follows GNOME HIG or provides distro-appropriate theming
Opens any markdown file (.md, .markdown, .mdown, .mkd, .mkdn, .mdx) via native file dialog
App launches and runs natively on Ubuntu 24.04 and Fedora 40
Local AI runs on Linux with NVIDIA or AMD GPU

items.title: F-020: Folder Browsing Sidebar
items.intent: Opt-in convenience for users who work within a project folder — never required, never a vault
items.description: Optional sidebar showing contents of a bookmarked directory. Not a vault — just a file browser convenience. Opt-in, not shown by default. User can bookmark multiple directories. Never required for any workflow.
acceptanceCriteria: User bookmarks a folder and sees its .md files in a sidebar
Sidebar is opt-in, not shown by default
All workflows work without the sidebar

items.title: F-021: Snippet/Template System
items.intent: Let users insert predefined markdown structures quickly — meeting notes, blog post scaffolds, table templates
items.description: Insert predefined markdown structures from a template library. Ship with a few built-in templates. Users can create custom snippets. Invoked via command palette or keyboard shortcut.
acceptanceCriteria: User invokes snippet picker and inserts a predefined markdown structure
User can create and save custom snippets

items.title: F-022a: Mermaid Diagram Rendering
items.intent: AI agents and builders produce documents with diagrams constantly. If a Mermaid block renders as raw text, the document experience is broken. Diagrams must render beautifully inline.
items.description: Render Mermaid diagram blocks as visual diagrams inline in the editor. Fenced code blocks with mermaid language tag render as visual diagrams in rich view. Supported types: flowcharts, sequence, ER, class, Gantt, pie, state, mind maps. Diagrams adapt to light/dark theme. Included in PDF/print export as vector graphics. The Render animation applies. Malformed Mermaid shows raw code with doctor warning. Depends on: FEAT-038 (Parser).
acceptanceCriteria: User opens a file with a Mermaid flowchart — it renders as a visual diagram inline
All 8 supported Mermaid diagram types render correctly
Diagram adapts to light/dark theme
Malformed Mermaid shows raw code with doctor warning
Toggle to source shows Mermaid code, toggle back re-renders diagram
PDF export includes rendered diagrams as vector graphics
Diagram renders within 500ms of file open
VoiceOver reads diagram type and text labels
items.groomingNotes: Root cause of 4 failures: MermaidRenderer.htmlTemplate loads mermaid.js from cdn.jsdelivr.net — this CDN reference fails in a sandboxed iOS app (no outbound network access during tests/runtime). Fix before anything else. CRITICAL PATH: (1) Bundle mermaid.min.js v10 as a local resource: download mermaid.min.js from the mermaid-js/mermaid v10 GitHub release, place at Sources/EMEditor/Resources/mermaid.min.js, add resources: [.process("Resources")] to the EMEditor target in Package.swift — verify with swift build. (2) Update MermaidRenderer.htmlTemplate: replace the CDN script tag with an inline script loaded via Bundle.module.url(forResource: "mermaid.min", withExtension: "js") — read the JS file content and embed it inline in the HTML string. (3) Integrate MermaidRenderer into MarkdownRenderer.renderCodeBlock: when language == "mermaid" and config.isSourceView == false, skip the existing syntax-highlighting path; instead show a placeholder NSTextAttachment (same pattern as broken-image placeholder in renderLoadedImage) and fire Task { @MainActor in let result = await mermaidRenderer.render(mermaidSource: content, theme: config.colorVariant == "dark" ? .dark : .light) — then trigger a re-render pass to replace the placeholder. (4) When MermaidRenderResult.failure is returned: apply standard code block styling (no attachment) and post a Diagnostic with .warning severity and message "Invalid Mermaid syntax: <error>". (5) Add MermaidRenderer as a stored property on MarkdownRenderer (lazy, created on first use). Call invalidateCache() when config.colorVariant changes. (6) Add WKWebView to window hierarchy for takeSnapshot: UIApplication.shared.connectedScenes' first UIWindowScene's keyWindow — addSubview(webView) at frame CGRect(x: -9999, y: -9999, ...) then removeFromSuperview after snapshot. Without this, takeSnapshot returns black/blank on some iOS versions. (7) Add webViewWebContentProcessDidTerminate: in NavigationDelegate, implement webViewWebContentProcessDidTerminate — set MermaidRenderer.webView = nil so next render re-creates it. (8) VoiceOver: set NSTextAttachment.accessibilityLabel to "Mermaid diagram: <first line of source>" using NSMutableAttributedString.addAttribute(.accessibilityLabel, ...). (9) Source view toggle: in source view, renderCodeBlock applies normal code block styling (language badge shows 'mermaid') — no attachment. (10) Tests: MermaidRendererTests should verify cache hit/miss, theme cache separation, and failure path. Integration test in MarkdownRenderer: render a document with a mermaid block and confirm an NSTextAttachment is set. Package build must pass: swift build. NOTE: FEAT-085 (bundle mermaid.js locally) must be done first — it is now listed as a dependency.

items.title: F-023: Shortcuts/Siri Integration
items.intent: Deep Apple platform integration — let users create new notes, open recent files, and trigger actions via Shortcuts and Siri
items.description: iOS Shortcuts integration: create new file, open recent file, open specific file. Siri: voice commands for common actions. Requires iOS/macOS apps to be shipped and stable first.
acceptanceCriteria: User creates a Shortcut that opens a specific .md file in easy-markdown
Siri can create a new markdown file via voice command

items.title: F-024: Quick Capture Widget
items.intent: Capture a thought instantly from the home screen without opening the app — lowest friction path to a new note
items.description: iOS home screen widget for quick capture. Tap to create a new markdown file and start typing immediately. Requires iOS app to be shipped and stable.
acceptanceCriteria: User taps home screen widget and is editing a new markdown file within 2 seconds
Widget works without the app being open in the foreground

items.title: F-029: AI — Generate from Prompt (Pro AI)
items.intent: Let users describe what they want and have AI generate the markdown — README scaffolds, comparison tables, outlines
items.description: Cloud-powered Pro AI feature. User types a prompt like 'write a README for a React component library' or 'create a table comparing AWS vs GCP storage'. AI generates markdown inserted at cursor. Inline preview before accepting.
acceptanceCriteria: User types a generation prompt and receives structured markdown output within 5 seconds
Generated content appears as preview before being inserted

items.title: F-030: AI — Document-Level Analysis (Pro AI)
items.intent: Let users ask the AI about their entire document — what is missing, suggest an outline, identify gaps in coverage
items.description: Cloud-powered Pro AI feature. User can ask document-level questions: 'what is missing from this doc?', 'suggest an outline', 'identify gaps'. Requires sending document content to cloud with explicit user consent per D-AI-8.
acceptanceCriteria: User asks what is missing from their document and receives actionable suggestions
User explicitly consents before document content is sent to cloud

items.title: F-031: AI — OCR + Image-to-Markdown
items.intent: Paste a screenshot and get markdown — tables extracted as markdown tables, text extracted as paragraphs
items.description: Paste screenshot or image, AI extracts text and tables as markdown. Could run locally (vision model) or via Pro AI cloud depending on hardware/model capability. Tables extracted as markdown tables, text as paragraphs.
acceptanceCriteria: User pastes a screenshot of a table — AI extracts it as a formatted markdown table
User pastes a screenshot of text — AI extracts it as markdown paragraphs

items.title: F-032: UI Localization
items.intent: The defacto global markdown editor needs to speak the user's language — localize the app interface to top 10 languages
items.description: Translate app interface (menus, buttons, settings, error messages, doctor suggestions) to top 10 languages. Content editing already supports all languages per D-USER-4. This is UI chrome localization only.
acceptanceCriteria: App interface displays correctly in all 10 supported languages
All strings including doctor suggestions and AI action labels are localized

items.title: F-022b: AI-Assisted Diagram Editing
items.intent: AI is the interface for editing diagrams — select a diagram, describe a change in natural language, AI rewrites the code and the diagram re-renders. No visual editor needed.
items.description: When user selects or taps a rendered Mermaid diagram, a contextual action bar appears with Edit with AI. User describes change in natural language. AI rewrites the Mermaid code, diagram re-renders. Shows inline diff with rendered preview. Uses same AI infrastructure as F-025. Depends on: FEAT-030 (Mermaid Rendering), FEAT-041 (AI Model Pipeline).
acceptanceCriteria: User taps a rendered flowchart, describes a change, AI updates the Mermaid code and diagram re-renders
Inline diff shows before/after Mermaid code alongside rendered preview
User can accept, edit manually, or dismiss the AI suggestion
Works with local AI for simple changes, Pro AI for complex modifications
Undo reverts the diagram to its pre-AI state
items.groomingNotes: BLOCKED: Depends on FEAT-030 (Mermaid Diagram Rendering) which is not yet done. Once FEAT-030 ships: add tap gesture on rendered WKWebView diagram to surface contextual AI action bar. Pass Mermaid source + user description to AI pipeline (FEAT-041). Render AI-rewritten Mermaid alongside inline diff. Accept replaces source in document; undo reverts.

items.title: F-022c: Extended Syntax Rendering (KaTeX, Footnotes)
items.intent: Users encounter math blocks and footnotes in existing files — we should render them even if we do not generate them
items.description: Render-only support for: KaTeX/LaTeX math blocks (inline and display), footnotes. We render these when present but never generate or auto-format them. No proprietary syntax. Per D-MD-2 Phase 3.
acceptanceCriteria: KaTeX math blocks render as formatted equations inline and in display mode
Footnotes render with superscript references and a footnotes section at bottom
Editor never generates these syntaxes, only renders them

items.title: GitHub Storage — Clone and Open
items.intent: Users can open markdown files directly from their GitHub repos with the same ease as opening from iCloud or Dropbox
items.description: Phase 2. 'Open from GitHub' option on home screen alongside 'Open File'. User authenticates via GitHub OAuth device flow (one-time). Browses their repos, selects a file. EMGit clones the repo (or pulls if already cloned) to a local app container directory. File opens in the editor via EMFile — editing is identical to any local file. Cloned repos cached locally; repos not accessed in 30 days offered for cleanup in Settings.
acceptanceCriteria: User taps Open from GitHub, authenticates once, browses repos, and opens a .md file
File opens in the editor and is editable immediately — identical to opening from iCloud
Re-opening the same file uses the local clone without re-cloning
Clone progress shown for first-time repos
User without GitHub account sees no GitHub option (graceful absence)
Large repo (>100MB) shows progress and does not freeze the UI
items.groomingNotes: Previous failure: SwiftGit2 SPM dependency was not added to Package.swift before attempting clone implementation. FEAT-084 (add SwiftGit2 to Package.swift) must run first — it is now listed as a dependency. Once FEAT-084 is done, skip step 1 of the original steps (dependency is already added). STEPS: (1) Create Sources/EMGit/EMGitCloneManager.swift: public actor EMGitCloneManager. Method: clone(repoURL: URL, to localPath: URL, token: String, progress: ((Double) -> Void)?) async throws — uses Repository.clone(from: repoURL, to: localPath) with Credentials.plaintext(username: "x-access-token", password: token). Call progress callback from libgit2 progress callback. If localPath already exists, call Repository(url: localPath) and pull instead. (2) Add CloneManifest to EMGit: struct CloneManifest: Codable { let ownerRepo: String; let localPath: URL; var lastAccessedAt: Date }. Persist to UserDefaults key "emgit.cloneManifest" as JSON array. EMGitCloneManager.recordAccess(ownerRepo:) updates lastAccessedAt. (3) Add "Open from GitHub" entry point in HomeView.swift (EMApp): below the existing Open File button. Show only if GitHubOAuthManager.shared.hasStoredToken() OR show always with 'Connect GitHub' flow if not authenticated. On tap: present RepoBrowserView (already implemented in FEAT-072). (4) On file selection in RepoBrowserView: call EMGitCloneManager.clone() — show ProgressView with "Cloning repo…" label during clone. On success, open file via FileOpenCoordinator (same path as Open File). Detect large repos (size > 100_000 KB from GitHubRepository.sizeKB) and show estimated time: "Large repo (~Xs)". (5) In Settings: list stale clones (lastAccessedAt > 30 days) with a 'Clean Up' button that deletes the local directory and removes from manifest. (6) Test: EMGitTests — mock Repository to verify clone path is Documents/GitRepos/{owner}/{repo}, re-open uses pull not clone, manifest is updated. Swift build must pass.

items.title: GitHub Storage — Auto-Commit and Push
items.intent: Changes to files in GitHub repos are automatically committed and pushed so the user never has to think about git operations
items.description: Phase 2. When user closes a file or the app backgrounds: auto-save fires, then EMGit commits the change ('Update filename.md') and pushes to the remote. Pull-with-rebase before push to detect conflicts. If push fails (network, conflict): non-modal banner, changes remain committed locally and push on next opportunity. Subtle toolbar indicator shows when there are unpushed local commits. User can tap the indicator to push manually at any time without closing the file. Conflict on pull surfaces via the same external-change flow as file conflict detection (FEAT-045).
acceptanceCriteria: User edits a file from a GitHub repo, backgrounds the app — changes are committed and pushed automatically
Push failure shows non-modal banner — changes are safe in local commit
Unpushed changes indicator appears in toolbar when local commits exist
User can tap indicator to push manually before closing
Conflict during pull shows reload-or-keep prompt (same as FEAT-045)
Auto-commit message is sensible: 'Update filename.md'
Network offline: changes accumulate locally, push when back online
items.groomingNotes: BLOCKED: Depends on FEAT-070 (GitHub Clone and Open) which is not yet done. Once FEAT-070 ships: hook into EMFile save path to detect files inside Documents/GitRepos/. On save: EMGit.commit('Update filename.md') then push. Pull-with-rebase before push. Toolbar indicator (unpushed badge) via published var on EMGitStatus. Conflict surfaces via existing FEAT-045 flow.

items.title: INFRA: Add mbernson/SwiftGit2 SPM dependency to Package.swift
items.intent: EMGit must compile with SwiftGit2 available before any git operation can be implemented
items.description: EMGit currently has no libgit2/SwiftGit2 dependency in Package.swift — it is a dependency-free target. All git clone/commit/push work in FEAT-070 and FEAT-071 requires this. Add mbernson/SwiftGit2 (SPM, compiles libgit2 from source, iOS 15.5+, no pre-built binaries). Verify swift build passes before marking done.
steps: Add .package(url: "https://github.com/mbernson/SwiftGit2", branch: "master") to the dependencies array in Package.swift
Add .product(name: "SwiftGit2", package: "SwiftGit2") to the EMGit target dependencies list in Package.swift
Run swift build and resolve any SPM fetch or compile errors
Run swift test to confirm no regressions
acceptanceCriteria: swift build succeeds with SwiftGit2 resolvable and EMGit target compiling
import SwiftGit2 compiles without errors in any EMGit source file
swift test passes with no regressions
items.groomingNotes: STEPS: (1) In Package.swift, add to dependencies array: .package(url: "https://github.com/mbernson/SwiftGit2", branch: "master"). (2) Update the EMGit target in Package.swift to add .product(name: "SwiftGit2", package: "SwiftGit2") to its dependencies array. Disable SSH/libssh2/OpenSSL — check if mbernson/SwiftGit2 has build flags for this; if not, that's acceptable (binary size is still 2-4MB as per SPIKE-009). (3) Run swift build — ensure it compiles cleanly. Fix any SPM resolution errors. (4) Run swift test — no tests should regress. (5) Commit Package.swift and Package.resolved. AC: swift build succeeds with SwiftGit2 resolvable and EMGit target compiles; Repository type from SwiftGit2 is importable in a new EMGit file.

items.title: DEBT: MermaidRenderer — replace CDN mermaid.js with bundled local resource
items.intent: mermaid.js must be bundled locally so Mermaid rendering works in a sandboxed app with no network access
items.description: MermaidRenderer.htmlTemplate loads mermaid.js from cdn.jsdelivr.net. In a sandboxed iOS app this CDN request fails at runtime and in tests, causing every Mermaid render to silently fail. Per SPIKE-006 recommendation: bundle mermaid.js v10 as a local resource in EMEditor. This is a standalone fix scoped to MermaidRenderer — no rendering pipeline changes needed.
steps: Download mermaid.min.js from the mermaid-js/mermaid v10 GitHub Releases page (latest v10.x) and save to Sources/EMEditor/Resources/mermaid.min.js
Add resources: [.process("Resources")] to the EMEditor target in Package.swift (EMCore already uses .copy("Resources/Fonts") as a reference pattern)

In MermaidRenderer.htmlTemplate, replace the CDN script tag (<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/..."></script>) with an inline <script> block: read the file using Bundle.module.url(forResource: "mermaid.min", withExtension: "js"), load its contents as a String, and embed it inline in the HTML — if the file is missing, fall back to an empty string and log an error
Run swift build to confirm Bundle.module resource access compiles correctly
Run swift test — MermaidRendererTests.testRenderFlowchart should now pass without network access
acceptanceCriteria: swift build succeeds with mermaid.min.js bundled as a Package resource
MermaidRenderer.render() returns .success for a simple flowchart when the device has no internet access
No CDN or external URLs appear in MermaidRenderer source after this change
items.groomingNotes: Elevated to must: this is a direct prerequisite for FEAT-030 (must-priority, 4 failures). The CDN load is the root cause of every FEAT-030 failure. This must ship before FEAT-030 is attempted again. Scope is narrow and self-contained — no pipeline changes, just bundle the asset and update MermaidRenderer.htmlTemplate.

items.title: DEBT: Implement EMGit.push() via libgit2 git_push API
items.intent: EMGit must be able to push commits to GitHub before FEAT-071 can be implemented
items.description: SwiftGit2 (mbernson fork) covers clone, commit, and fetch but does not wrap libgit2's push API. SPIKE-009 explicitly calls for adding push support (~50 lines, reference in SwiftGit3). This is a standalone, testable addition to EMGit that unblocks FEAT-071 (GitHub Auto-Commit and Push). Depends on FEAT-084 (SwiftGit2 SPM dependency).
steps: Create Sources/EMGit/Repository+Push.swift as a Swift extension on Repository (from SwiftGit2)

Implement push(remote: String = "origin", token: String) async throws: call git_push_new, git_push_add_refspec ("refs/heads/main:refs/heads/main" or detect current branch), git_push_set_callbacks with credential callback using Credentials.plaintext(username: "x-access-token", password: token), git_push_finish, then git_push_unpack_ok to check for server-side errors — see SwiftGit3's GitPush.swift as reference implementation (~50 lines of C API calls via libgit2)
Map libgit2 error codes to EMError.git cases: authenticationExpired for 401, pushRejected(reason:) for non-fast-forward, networkError for unreachable remote
Add EMGitTests/PushTests.swift: unit tests using a local bare repository (init a bare repo in temp dir, clone it, commit a file, push — verify the bare repo has the commit). No network required.
Run swift test — PushTests must pass
acceptanceCriteria: Repository.push() compiles and is callable from EMGit
PushTests: push to a local bare repo succeeds with correct commit reaching the remote
Push with invalid credentials throws EMError.git(.authenticationExpired)
swift test passes with no regressions

items.title: DEBT: MermaidRenderer — add WKWebView process termination recovery
items.intent: MermaidRenderer must recover from iOS killing the WKWebView process without user-visible failure
items.description: SPIKE-006 risk table calls out: 'WKWebView process killed under memory pressure (reuse) — Medium likelihood on 6GB devices'. The current MermaidRenderer has no webViewWebContentProcessDidTerminate handler, so if iOS kills the web content process the renderer silently fails on all subsequent renders. Add the recovery handler so the WKWebView is transparently re-created on the next render call.
steps: In MermaidRenderer.swift, add a WKNavigationDelegate extension method webViewWebContentProcessDidTerminate(_:) to the private NavigationDelegate class

In the termination handler, post a notification or call a closure so MermaidRenderer learns the web view process died — set MermaidRenderer.webView = nil (since MermaidRenderer is @MainActor, this is safe from the delegate callback which also runs on main thread)
Verify that the next call to render() re-creates the WKWebView via the existing createWebView() path (webView is nil, so the guard in renderInWebView recreates it)
Add a test in MermaidRendererTests: create a renderer, manually call webViewWebContentProcessDidTerminate, then call render() and verify it returns a result (not a crash or permanent failure)
Run swift test to confirm the recovery test passes
acceptanceCriteria: After webViewWebContentProcessDidTerminate fires, MermaidRenderer.webView is nil
The next render() call succeeds (creates a new WKWebView) — no permanent failure state
swift test passes for the recovery test case

_dependsOn_docs: Items may include an optional 'dependsOn' array of item ID strings (e.g. "dependsOn": ["FEAT-001"]). Auto-pick skips items whose dependencies are not yet done.
